danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct enemy car sprites moving toward player on vertical scrolling road
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring evasion or destruction

danger_signals.visual_form: Airborne sprite dropping animated bomb indicators onto road surface
danger_signals.meaning: Overhead threat creating destructible road hazards (craters)

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated tiles showing road deformation where helicopter bombs land
danger_signals.meaning: Collision hazard that destroys player vehicle

danger_signals.visual_form: Player car sprite outside road boundaries (terrain or water edges)
danger_signals.meaning: Damage state with progressive destruction as distance increases

danger_signals.visual_form: Vehicle sprite contact with enemy or obstacle, accompanied by state change
danger_signals.meaning: Hard collision destroys player car; forces off-road lead to destruction

safety_signals.visual_form: Continuous scrolling asphalt/road tiles in forward direction
safety_signals.meaning: Safe travel zone; score accumulation active

safety_signals.visual_form: Non-hostile car sprites on road, distinct from enemy vehicles
safety_signals.meaning: Protection objective; destruction halts score counter temporarily

safety_signals.visual_form: Special vehicle sprite appearing periodically in each territory
safety_signals.meaning: Entry opportunity grants special weapon pickup (oil slick, smoke screen, missiles)

safety_signals.visual_form: UI lights flashing on dashboard when weapon button available
safety_signals.meaning: Special weapon ready to summon via button press

safety_signals.visual_form: Structure at road edge allowing vehicle-to-speedboat transformation
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary mode shift providing altered tactical landscape

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Controlled vehicle sprite; occupies center-screen position in top-down view

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinct enemy car sprite designs (variety of vehicle types)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy vehicle types with different visual profiles; helicopter as aerial variant
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Approach from directions on scrolling road; helicopter overhead; spawn from road edges

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Non-hostile car sprites visually distinct from enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Travel on road alongside player; protected status indicated by non-engagement behavior

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Horizontal road scrolling vertically; standard terrain background; typical weather conditions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard enemy vehicle attacks; machine gun engagement viable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Animated background tiles change; road coloring or texture shifts; environmental detail shifts (grass, water proximity)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New enemy vehicle types may appear; tactical options change based on terrain

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Background tile animation indicates weather state (rain, snow, or storm visual effects via CHR bank animation)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Road conditions may affect vehicle handling or visibility; enemy behavior patterns may shift

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player sprite transforms from car to speedboat; road sprite becomes water; visual palette shifts to aquatic theme
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy boats replace car enemies; helicopter still present; oil slick becomes flamethrower; movement physics change

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dashboard UI lights illuminate; weapon indicator lights flash on cabinet status display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains access to limited-use weapon (oil slick, smoke screen, missiles); ammunition counter decreases on use

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player car sprite vanishes or shows destruction animation; extra life counter decrements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game resets player position; score counter halts if caused by non-enemy collision; special weapons lost

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Score counter reaches target; visual feedback or UI change signals extra car earned
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Extra car awarded; player continues indefinitely rather than entering 'game over' state

environmental_storytelling.example: Periodic road forks present player with directional choice leading to new territories with distinct visual themes (different terrain, weather, or landscape features)
Each new region accessed via road fork displays distinct animated background tiles and road coloring, communicating geographical progression without narrative text
Special vehicle appears in each territory as visual landmark; its presence communicates 'support infrastructure' within world without exposition
Visual building at road edge signals mode transition point; presence indicates hybrid land-water environment without explicit instruction
CHR bank animation numbers embedded in Stage 5-6 tiles create illusion of environmental change (weather, time, or damage) through sprite cycling
Non-hostile cars on road communicate 'safe passage corridor' within larger conflict; their movement patterns show normal traffic flow contrasting with aggressive enemy behavior

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Approaches player vehicle with intent to collide or force off-road; sprite moves toward player center-screen position
Sprite disappears from road; score awarded to player; vehicle removes from threat pool
Sprite remains overhead; animated bomb drops create crater hazards on road surface below; appears in multiple territories
Moves on road without attacking; collision with player halts score counter temporarily but does not destroy player vehicle
Appears periodically in each territory; sprite can be entered to grant special weapon; dashboard light flashes to indicate summon availability
Takes progressive damage as distance from road increases; sprite visually outside road boundary; eventual destruction if not corrected
Dashboard lights illuminate to show active weapon type; sprite gains visual firing indicators when special weapons activated
Replaces car enemies during water sections; attacks from front or rear on water sprite; helicopter continues overhead threat

notes: Super Spy Hunter (NES, 1992) is a vertical-scrolling driving game with strong visual communication of threat levels through enemy vehicle types, road states, and environmental hazards. The game uses animated tile sequences (CHR bank cycling) to convey weather and terrain changes without explicit text. Faction distinction is minimal (player vs. generic enemies vs. civilians), but visual sprite differentiation communicates allegiance. The weapons van acts as environmental storytelling—its presence signals support infrastructure. Dashboard UI lights function as manifestation of game state (weapon availability, score milestones). The game lacks narrative exposition, relying entirely on visual and behavioral cues to communicate danger, safety, progression, and world-state shifts. Road fork branching and territory-specific backgrounds suggest geographical narrative without dialogue or text.
